title: nie-hlns W1A: Relay handler rewrite to JSON-RPC
description:
## What
Rewrite relay/src/ws.rs to speak JSON-RPC 2.0 instead of the RelayMessage tag-discriminated format. Relay binary must compile and pass all existing relay tests after this bead.

## Algorithm
1. Replace all outgoing RelayMessage constructions with JsonRpcNotification or JsonRpcResponse:
   - Auth handshake:
     a. Send Challenge as: JsonRpcNotification::new(CHALLENGE, ChallengeParams{nonce})?.to_json_string()
     b. Parse incoming frame as JsonRpcRequest; validate id field (must be u64, not null)
     c. If method != AUTHENTICATE: send error response {id, code: NOT_AUTHENTICATED}; close
     d. Deserialize AuthenticateParams from request.params; call verify_challenge()
     e. On success: send JsonRpcResponse::success(id, AuthenticateResult{...})
     f. On failure: send JsonRpcResponse::error(id, AUTH_FAILED, reason); close
   - After auth: send DirectoryList as JsonRpcNotification::new(DIRECTORY_LIST, DirectoryListParams{...})
   - Send UserJoined as JsonRpcNotification for each existing peer
2. In the main dispatch loop, replace match on RelayMessage with:
   a. Parse each text frame as JsonRpcRequest (may fail → warn!+continue, send error -32700)
   b. Check version == "2.0" (else error -32600)
   c. Match on request.method:
      - BROADCAST: check subscription; deserialize BroadcastParams; relay-set from = authenticated pub_id; broadcast DeliverParams as notification to all except sender; respond JsonRpcResponse::success(id, BroadcastResult{message_id: Uuid::new_v4().to_string()})
      - SET_NICKNAME: deserialize SetNicknameParams; validate 1-32 chars; call try_set_nickname; success → respond ok + broadcast UserNickname notification; fail → respond error NICKNAME_TAKEN
      - PUBLISH_KEY_PACKAGE: deserialize PublishKeyPackageParams; save_key_package().await; respond ok; broadcast KeyPackageReady notification ONLY AFTER save_key_package() resolves
      - GET_KEY_PACKAGE: deserialize GetKeyPackageParams; get_key_package(); respond JsonRpcResponse::success(id, GetKeyPackageResult{...})
      - WHISPER: deserialize WhisperParams; relay-set from = authenticated pub_id (never client-supplied); deliver_live(to, WhisperDeliver notification)
      - SUBSCRIBE_REQUEST: respond error METHOD_NOT_FOUND for now (Phase 3)
      - unknown method: respond error METHOD_NOT_FOUND (-32601)
3. Broadcast path: all fan-out messages are JsonRpcNotification (no id); state.broadcast() takes String (serialized notification) not RelayMessage
4. Remove all RelayMessage constructions from relay/src/ws.rs
5. Update state.rs deliver_live/broadcast signatures from RelayMessage to String (pre-serialized JSON)

## Defensive programming rules
- Never accept 'from' field from client in Broadcast or Whisper params — relay always sets from
- Validate request id is present and u64 before processing any request
- Gate all methods on authenticated flag (bool set after AuthOk sent)
- PUBLISH_KEY_PACKAGE must await save_key_package() before broadcasting KeyPackageReady
- All outgoing JSON: use helper methods, not hand-rolled strings
- Log sender-identity check failures with warn! (spoofed_sender code)
- Never deserialize payload bytes in relay — pass through as Vec<u8>

## Agent team: Critical-path (R + I_A + I_B + T + DIFF)
- R: Read relay/src/ws.rs and relay/src/state.rs in full; produce context summary
- I_A: Implement relay rewrite using R's summary
- I_B: Implement relay rewrite independently using R's summary
- T: Write test suite asserting relay sends valid JSON-RPC; test unauthenticated method rejection, spoofed-sender rejection, KeyPackageReady ordering; use external oracle (two live clients)
- DIFF: Compare I_A and I_B outputs; if they agree on the five security invariants, merge one

## Tests (security-critical — T writes before seeing implementation)
- unauthenticated_broadcast_rejected: connect, skip auth, send broadcast request → expect error -32002
- spoofed_from_in_params_ignored: send broadcast with extra 'from' field in params → delivered message has from=authenticated_pub_id not the spoofed value
- key_package_ready_after_write: publish KP, capture ready notification → verify KP fetchable via get_key_package
- error_response_has_matching_id: send broadcast with id=42 when not subscribed → error response has id=42
acceptance_criteria: cargo build -p nie-relay passes; relay binary sends valid JSON-RPC 2.0 only; no RelayMessage constructions remain in relay/src/ws.rs; clippy clean; security invariants tested
